content: Combining the task-agnostic iterative self-feedback framework of Self-Refine with multiple-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms reveals that Self-Refine can be modified to inject inconsistent problem-solving perspectives into each iteration of self-feedback, rather than only refining a single initial output along a single reasoning path. This modified framework would produce more robust, less biased outputs without requiring any additional training or external supervision, preserving the core advantage of original Self-Refine.
